pr.title: Run `ninja diff` and `ninja all_source` by default
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Unmerged tooling PR that proposed changing the generated Ninja default targets so that, when a build configuration is active, plain `ninja` would also run `diff` and `all_source` in addition to the existing progress target. The only code change was two added `n.default(...)` calls in `tools/project.py` inside `add_unit`. There was no PR body, discussion, or review feedback in the slice, and the PR was closed without merge shortly after creation.
what_changed: In `tools/project.py`, inside `add_unit(build_obj: Dict[str, Any], link_step: LinkStep)`, the generated Ninja file's default rule was extended under the `if build_config:` branch.
The existing default target `progress_path` remained in place.
Two additional default targets were proposed: `diff` and `all_source`.
The `else` branch for no active build configuration still defaulted to `build_config_path` unchanged.
smart_moves: The proposed change was very small and localized to Ninja generation logic rather than scattering command behavior elsewhere.
The change only affected the `build_config` path, preserving the existing behavior where users without a selected build config are directed to generate or select the build configuration first.
Using `n.default("diff")` and `n.default("all_source")` reused existing Ninja targets instead of introducing wrapper scripts or duplicate build logic.
decomp_lessons: Default build targets can influence contributor workflow significantly; making `diff` a default target would surface matching regressions automatically when running plain `ninja`.

Adding `all_source` to defaults suggests an intent to keep generated or expected source aggregation up to date as part of normal builds, but the PR provides no explanation of the intended workflow impact.
Tooling changes that alter default command behavior should be justified clearly because they can affect developer iteration speed and CI-like local workflows.
Closed-unmerged PRs should not be treated as accepted project practice unless corroborated by later merged changes.
naming_conventions: Existing Ninja target names are lowercase snake/camel-free identifiers such as `diff` and `all_source`.
The project.py generator uses variables such as `progress_path` and `build_config_path` for file-like default targets and string literals for named Ninja targets.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: This PR did not touch assembly, C matching code, or symbol-level decompilation tactics.
The relevant matching-adjacent tactic is workflow-oriented: running the `diff` target by default would make match-status checking part of ordinary local builds.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR was closed without merge; the slice does not state why.
follow_up_queries: Look for later merged PRs that modify `tools/project.py` default Ninja targets.
Search for discussion around making `diff` or `all_source` default Ninja targets.
Check whether CI or contributor documentation later recommends `ninja diff` or `ninja all_source` explicitly.
